title: Young Hero Wins Nobel Peace Prize
story: A teenage girl became a global champion for education in 2014. Malala Yousafzai, just 17 years old, was awarded the Nobel Peace Prize on October 10. She shared this honor with Kailash Satyarthi, an activist fighting child labor. Malala's bravery in speaking up for girls' right to learn, even after surviving an attack, inspired millions worldwide. Her win made her the youngest Nobel laureate ever.
activity_question: Who did Malala Yousafzai share the Nobel Peace Prize with?
follow_up_question: What cause was Malala fighting for?

story: Skates flashing, Real Cloutier burst onto the ice for his first-ever NHL game. title: Grandpa Runs Marathon at 98
story: Sneakers laced tight, Dimitrion Yordanidis took his mark at the starting line. At 98 years old, this Greek runner wasn't your typical marathoner. In 1976, Yordanidis became the oldest person ever to compete in a marathon. Showing incredible endurance, he finished the grueling 26.2-mile race in about seven and a half hours. His determination proved that age is just a number when it comes to pursuing your passions.

follow_up_question: What sport would you like to try when youre older?

story: Londoners buzzed with excitement in 1684. A strange beast had arrived in their city! Posters went up on October 10, urging folks to visit a tavern on Ludgate Hill. title: Triton: Neptune's Icy Moon Revealed
story: Stargazers, get ready for a cosmic discovery! In 1846, astronomer William Lassell spotted something extraordinary through his telescope. He found Triton, a frozen moon orbiting the distant planet Neptune. This icy world, discovered on October 10, turned out to be one of the largest moons in our solar system. Triton's backward orbit makes it unique, spinning the opposite way most moons do!
activity_question: What makes Triton's orbit unique?
bonus_fact: Triton is so cold that its surface is mostly frozen nitrogen!

title: Tokyo's Olympic Triumph
story: Cheers erupted as the flame ignited, marking the start of the XVIII Summer Olympic Games in 1964. Tokyo's National Stadium buzzed with excitement on October 10. Athletes from around the world gathered, ready to showcase their skills. This event was special - it was the first Olympics held in Asia, putting Japan in the global spotlight. The games brought new technology too, with live satellite broadcasts beaming the action worldwide.

They had the most athletes ever

bonus_fact: These Olympics introduced judo as a new sport.

title: The Beach Boys' Sonic Revolution
story: Surf's up! The Beach Boys rode a wave of musical innovation in 1966. On October 10, they released 'Good Vibrations', a groundbreaking single that changed pop music forever. This wasn't just another catchy tune. It was a mini-symphony, crafted from multiple recording sessions and layered with unusual instruments like the otherworldly theremin. The song's complex structure and harmonies pushed the boundaries of what was possible in the recording studio.
activity_question: What unusual instrument was featured in 'Good Vibrations'?
bonus_fact: 'Good Vibrations' took months to record, using a technique called 'modular recording' - like building with musical Lego blocks!

title: Fiji's Freedom: A New Island Nation
story: Flags waved and cheers filled the air as Fiji became its own country in 1970. After nearly a century under British rule, the beautiful island nation in the South Pacific Ocean declared its independence on October 10. This historic moment marked the birth of a new nation, with its own government and unique blend of cultures. Fijians celebrated their freedom with traditional dances, feasts, and joyful ceremonies across the islands.
activity_question: What did Fiji gain on October 10, 1970?
bonus_fact: Fiji is made up of over 300 islands, but only about 100 are inhabited.

title: Boom! Waters Unite in Panama
story: Ka-boom! The Gamboa Dam in Panama exploded into pieces. This wasn't an accident, but a planned event in 1913. As the dam crumbled on October 10, something amazing happened. For the first time, waters from the Atlantic Ocean mixed with those from the Pacific Ocean. It was a big step in creating the Panama Canal, a waterway that would change shipping forever.
activity_question: What happened when the Gamboa Dam was blown up?
activity_choices: Waters from the Atlantic and Pacific mixed
activity_answer: Waters from the Atlantic and Pacific mixed
bonus_fact: The Panama Canal saves ships about 8,000 miles of travel around South America.

title: Cuba's Cry for Freedom
story: Shouts of 'Liberty!' echoed across Cuba in 1868. On October 10, brave islanders launched a revolt against Spanish rule. Led by Carlos Manuel de Céspedes, they declared independence and freed their slaves. This bold action sparked a decade-long struggle known as the Ten Years' War. Though it didn't immediately succeed, the revolt planted seeds of freedom that would eventually bloom.
activity_question: Who led the Cuban revolt for independence?
bonus_fact: The Cuban flag was first raised during this revolt, featuring a lone white star on a red triangle.

Little did anyone know that this Texas girl would grow up to dazzle audiences and win a Tony Award for her incredible performance in the musical 'Kimberly Akimbo' many years later.
activity_question: Where was Victoria Clark born?
follow_up_question: What award did Victoria Clark win for her performance in 'Kimberly Akimbo'?

title: From California to the Stars
story: Rex J. Walheim's journey to the stars began in Redwood City, California. Born on October 10, 1962, this future U.S. Air Force officer and NASA astronaut would grow up to explore the vastness of space. Walheim's incredible career would take him from the sunny West Coast to the darkness of orbit, inspiring countless kids to reach for the stars.
activity_question: Where was Rex J. Walheim born?
follow_up_question: What do you think it takes to become an astronaut?

title: Hong Kong's Pop Princess Arrives
story: A star was born in Hong Kong! On October 10, 1963, Anita Mui entered the world. She grew up to become a dazzling Cantopop singer and actress. Her talent and style were so impressive that people called her the 'Madonna of the East.' Mui's powerful voice and charismatic performances made her a beloved icon in Asian entertainment.
activity_question: What nickname was Anita Mui given?
bonus_fact: Cantopop is a style of popular music sung in Cantonese, a language spoken in Hong Kong.
